isolation.environment: local live daemon-event + broadcast validation (controlled stack)

assertions.title: Daemon-event cross-area flow is executable with authenticated test context

steps.action: Execute daemon-event route tests with the new test-auth seam
steps.expected: Test-context daemon auth is accepted in non-production, invalid/production paths are rejected, and no X-Daemon-Token bypass is allowed
steps.observed: Route tests passed with explicit coverage for valid test-auth, invalid secret rejection, production rejection, and token precedence rejection.

steps.action: Execute live terminal daemon-event path under test auth and capture the delivery-loop websocket refetch from a browser surface
steps.expected: Terminal v2 daemon-event should return 200 and publish `refetch:["delivery-loop"]` to the user channel within 500ms
steps.observed: On the controlled local stack (`web:3100`, `broadcast:2999`), an `agent-browser` harness opened the real user-channel websocket, posted a terminal v2 daemon-event, and waited specifically for the `refetch:["delivery-loop"]` frame. The browser capture recorded `postStatus=200`, `postElapsedMs=112.6`, `socketDeltaMs=112.9`, and `within500ms=true`, with frame preview `{"op":"refetch","refetch":["delivery-loop"]}`.

commands: pnpm -C apps/www exec vitest run src/app/api/daemon-event/route.test.ts
psql ... update agent_run_context/thread/thread_chat back to processing/working for isolated live fixture `val-cross-browser-run-3100`
agent-browser --session val-cross-browser eval <browser websocket + fetch timing harness filtered to refetch:["delivery-loop"]>
commandResults: PASS: browser websocket probe connected on ws://127.0.0.1:2999/parties/main/user:67DFFNToBBmnlKm4XPaTQ8QuBe6bWh2k?apiKey=<daemon-token>

summary: VAL-CROSS-001 is executable and passing with browser-backed live evidence: authenticated test-context daemon-event ingress completed end-to-end `/api/daemon-event` -> websocket `refetch:["delivery-loop"]`, and the browser-observed POST-to-frame delta stayed under the 500ms contract.
